authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public hospitality or travel booking guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal ADA Title III authority. The lodging reservation provisions directly constrain accessible room descriptions, reservation blocking, and guarantees for room availability and reservation intake.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessible room and accommodation prerequisite handling

authoritative_sources.title: Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public hospitality or travel booking guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal consumer-protection regulator. Its short-term lodging and live-event fee guidance is authoritative for mandatory-fee, total-price, and truthful fee-description evidence in lodging and package reservation intake.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: rate, package, fee, and offer evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: Passengers with Disabilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public hospitality or travel booking guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOT enforces air travel disability protections. Its public passenger guidance is directly relevant when travel booking intake includes accessible assistance, seating, service-animal, or special-service needs.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenTravel Specifications
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OpenTravel Alliance is a travel-industry standards body. Its specifications provide vendor-neutral hotel, air, rail, vehicle, package, payment, profile, and itinerary terminology that supports reservation evidence and system interoperability without implying supplier approval authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: reservation message and itinerary evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Eligibility Transaction System (HETS) 270/271
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS operates Medicare eligibility transaction infrastructure. HETS supports eligibility evidence, 270/271 terminology, and the boundary between an eligibility response and final booking or access clearance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility verification and covered-travel prerequisite handling

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule (CMS-0057-F)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS-0057-F is federal public policy for impacted payer prior authorization APIs, status transparency, and denial-reason communication. It supports authorization status workflow while preserving payer-decision boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization status readiness and payer-response handling

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. Minimum-necessary guidance directly constrains roster, eligibility, referral, authorization, lodging, travel, and escalation packets that contain PHI.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy-gated evidence packaging and requester handling

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Care Coordination
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance on treatment, payment, and health care operations is authoritative for PHI disclosure boundaries that may appear in healthcare-related reservation, covered lodging, and care travel workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI disclosure and health care operations boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: CAQH CORE Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE is designated by HHS as the operating-rule authoring entity for HIPAA administrative transactions and directly supports eligibility, prior authorization, referrals, attachments, acknowledgements, error handling, and healthcare revenue-cycle workflow terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, referral, prior-authorization, attachment, and acknowledgement workflow

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability Version 6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal interoperability baseline for patient demographics, encounter, care team, coverage, insurance, and clinical artifact terminology used when EHR-linked booking evidence must be normalized.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient demographics, coverage, encounter, and artifact terminology normalization

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Minimum prerequisites include reservation request identity, sponsor or payer where applicable, stay or travel dates, arrival/departure pattern, origin/destination, party size, room type or booking class, rate plan, itinerary components, accessible accommodation or special-assistance needs, payment or deposit dependency, rate and fee evidence, hold/ticketing/cut-off/release deadlines, cancellation and change terms, payer/plan context where applicable, eligibility status, referral status, authorization status, service date and setting, requester role, consent/privacy posture, owner readiness, source hierarchy, and retrieval hooks.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Healthcare truth-set systems include EHR/EMR, practice management, payer portals, real-time eligibility, referral, authorization, case management, utilization review, CRM/patient access, document repositories, privacy/consent systems, and audit logs. Hospitality and travel truth-set systems include PMS, CRS, GDS, booking engine, OTA/channel manager, travel management, reservation CRM, PNR or locator, confirmation, folio, payment/deposit, itinerary, guest/traveler profile, and special-service records. Tenant source hierarchy determines controlling records when systems conflict.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical urgency, medical necessity, utilization review judgment, authorization approval, payer coverage interpretation, privacy-release approval, legal interpretation, rate or contract approval, concession or refund approval, and safety-critical reservation execution. Those require adjacent owner escalation or orchestrator return.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and lane classification
workflow_stages.description: Normalize the requested lodging, air, rail, ground transport, package, tour, dining, amenity, accessible accommodation, healthcare-access lodging, or booking-readiness action before treating work as actionable.
source_classes: Public hospitality or travel booking guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Reservation prerequisite capture
workflow_stages.description: Capture stay or travel dates, origin/destination, party size, room type or booking class, rate plan, reservation inventory, traveler or guest names, PNR or locator, confirmation status, accessible-room or special-assistance needs, hold/ticketing/cut-off/release dates, deposit status, cancellation and change terms, booking channel, and owner readiness.
source_classes: Public hospitality or travel booking guidance

workflow_stages.name: Eligibility, referral, authorization, and privacy validation
workflow_stages.description: For healthcare-sensitive reservations, validate payer, plan, eligibility, referral, authorization, service date, service setting, consent, requester role, minimum-necessary PHI purpose, and privacy constraints before downstream travel or lodging work proceeds.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Source reconciliation and blocker classification
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile PMS, CRS, GDS, booking engine, OTA/channel manager, reservation operations, reservation CRM, travel management, EHR/EMR, payer portal, eligibility, authorization, referral, case management, utilization review, privacy, and document records; classify blockers as intake, inventory, accessibility, eligibility, authorization, referral, privacy, payment, capacity, system, policy, or owner-readiness related.
source_classes: Public hospitality or travel booking guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Disposition, routing, and controlled escalation
workflow_stages.description: Produce accepted, pended, rejected, routed, or blocked dispositions; create booking readiness summaries and escalation packets; route clinical, PHI, payer, accessibility, legal, rate, contract, capacity, and safety conflicts to authorized owners.
source_classes: Public hospitality or travel booking guidance

artifacts: Reservation request, reservation packet, RFP where applicable, rate plan, booking class, room type, itinerary, service package, PNR, reservation locator, confirmation, traveler or guest roster, linked reservations, stay pattern request, booking status report, hold/ticketing/cut-off/release deadlines, cancellation and change terms, deposit status, payment authorization, folio, and payment routing notes

Reservation confirmations, PMS records, CRS records, GDS records, booking-engine records, OTA/channel-manager records, reservation operations records, reservation CRM records, travel management records, folio and payment records, and guest/traveler communication notes
Accessible-room descriptions, accessibility requests, special-service requests, travel assistance needs, service-animal handling notes, and accessibility escalation evidence

Eligibility, benefits, 270/271 response, payer and plan identifiers, member ID, referral order, authorization record, authorization number, payer portal status, provider/facility/service setting, service date, and utilization-review case record

Encounter documentation, orders, clinical-administrative notes, discharge or care-transition records, case-management notes, charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal documentation when relevant to covered travel or lodging
Privacy, consent, proxy, requester-role, minimum-necessary, disclosure, audit, and communication-channel constraints
Intake disposition notes, prerequisite or eligibility checklists, booking readiness summaries, missing-prerequisite requests, escalation packets, and adjacent-specialist routing notes
systems_of_record: Global distribution system (GDS) and booking engine
Reservation CRM and reservation sales pipeline
Payment, deposit, payment-routing, folio, and authorization systems
EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
Practice management, patient/member access, and scheduling systems
Real-time eligibility and payer portal systems
Referral, prior authorization, case-management, and utilization-review systems
Revenue cycle, charge capture, coding, claim, denial, remittance, and appeal systems
Privacy, consent, document repository, communication, and audit systems

allowed: Classify reservation requests as accepted, pended, rejected, routed, or blocked before downstream commitments.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, and retrieval hooks.
Determine whether reservations, eligibility, referrals, authorizations, accessible-room prerequisites, payment/deposit dependencies, and owner approvals are present.
Prepare evidence-backed intake disposition notes, prerequisite checklists, readiness summaries, missing-item requests, and escalation packets.
Route work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, or human reviewer when ownership shifts.
escalate: Escalate unresolved source conflicts between PMS, CRS, reservation CRM, payer portal, EHR/EMR, authorization, referral, or privacy records.
Escalate PHI, consent, requester-role, proxy, or minimum-necessary uncertainty to healthcare privacy or care-team owners.
Escalate clinical urgency, medical necessity, licensed review, payer interpretation, or utilization-management issues.
Escalate accessible-room or travel-assistance conflicts that may implicate civil-rights or carrier obligations.
Escalate rate, contract, cancellation, cancellation, concession, refund, deposit, safety-critical, legal, or policy-override decisions.
refuse: Refuse to approve downstream booking work when required tenant facts, records, or authoritative source context are missing and would change the outcome.
Refuse requests to bypass eligibility, referral, authorization, privacy, accessibility, payment, deposit, or owner-readiness checks.

Refuse to imply diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical judgment, medical necessity, payer coverage determination, authorization approval, privacy-release approval, legal interpretation, financial approval, or safety signoff.
Refuse to disclose or package PHI beyond the minimum necessary purpose and approved channel.
Refuse confident synthesis when public sources, tenant policy, payer rules, supplier terms, or jurisdictional obligations materially conflict.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Group commitment accepted without complete traveler or guest roster, dates, inventory, accessible-room, deposit, or owner-readiness evidence
domain_failure_modes.impact: Overbooking, inaccessible accommodation, guest/member harm, service failure, revenue loss, or contract dispute
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Pend intake and issue prerequisite checklist with source-of-record and owner retrieval requirements.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Covered travel or lodging proceeds without eligibility, referral, or authorization evidence
domain_failure_modes.impact: Patient/member access failure, claim denial, revenue leakage, compliance exposure, or unsafe routing
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Block readiness and route missing eligibility, referral, or authorization to patient access, payer operations, or utilization-management owner.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: PHI from traveler, patient, or member roster is exposed in an unapproved channel or excessive escalation packet
domain_failure_modes.impact: Privacy violation, incomplete audit trail, loss of trust, or regulatory exposure
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Apply minimum-necessary handling and route unresolved consent, requester-role, or disclosure authority to healthcare privacy.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Administrative booking lane implies clinical judgment, medical necessity, or payer-policy interpretation
domain_failure_modes.impact: Unsafe care routing, unauthorized payer determination, delayed care, or compliance exposure
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Refuse clinical or payer-authority decision and route to licensed clinician, clinical review, utilization management, or payer operations.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: PMS, reservation operations, reservation CRM, EHR, or payer portal records conflict and the lane chooses one without source hierarchy
domain_failure_modes.impact: Wrong inventory commitment, incorrect authorization posture, duplicate booking, lost revenue, or patient/member harm
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Record the conflict, withhold readiness, and return to orchestrator or responsible owner for source hierarchy resolution.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Wrong-lane rate, contract, or safety request is handled as booking intake
domain_failure_modes.impact: Unauthorized financial commitment, contract exposure, unsafe reservation execution, or unsupported customer promise
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Create boundary-safe routing note and hand off to reservation sales, revenue management, legal, safety, or reservation operations.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Missing prerequisite or required intake data
Request routed to the wrong lane before execution starts
Attempt to bypass access, eligibility, or authorization checks
Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific payer, plan, sponsor, property, supplier, and jurisdiction rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Retrieve tenant policy, payer contract, property/brand standard, supplier term, jurisdiction, source hierarchy, and approval matrix before case-level disposition.
Route pricing, contract, concession, refund, cancellation, cancellation, deposit, and financial approval questions to authorized reservation sales, revenue management, legal, or finance owners.

Route diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical urgency, medical necessity, authorization approval, payer coverage interpretation, denial, appeal, and utilization-review questions to licensed clinical, utilization management, payer operations, or revenue cycle owners.

research_use_in_pack: Manifest boundaries require booking readiness rather than downstream booking approval.

Scenario set includes missing prerequisites, authorization/referral/eligibility blockers, PHI-sensitive roster handling, wrong-lane routing, bypass attempts, clinical/payer escalation, source conflicts, and tenant-specific adaptation.
Functionality map ties required shared, intake/access, and healthcare-profile abilities to passing scenarios.
Readiness evidence records validated status while preserving specification-based runtime strategy.
